request.messages: [redacted: candidate evidence omitted from raw judge artifact]

message.reasoning_content:
We are asked to validate extracted ASHRAE Guideline 36 items. We have a list of candidates, each with properties. We must output JSON with verdicts, one per candidate_id. For each, is_valid_guideline36_knowledge is true or false. If false, provide reason and category_if_not (from allowed list). We need to accept only if it is useful, grounded ASHRAE Guideline 36 operational knowledge. Reject legal/front matter, TOC text, generic definitions without operational use, duplicated/noisy extraction, or summaries not supported by evidence.

Let's go through each candidate:

cand_520edf20988f53b5: title "Dual-Duct VAV Zone Control Logic with Dual Inlet Airflow Sensors", section 5.11.5.1. Summary describes zone state behavior for cooling, deadband, heating. Evidence quote: "5.1.15.1.  Parallel equipment  shall be lead/lag or lead/standby rotated to maintain even wear." This evidence does not match the summary at all. The summary is about dual-duct VAV zone control, but the evidence is about parallel equipment rotation. Likely misattributed. Also source_pages: [50]. This seems like an incorrect extraction, the evidence is unrelated. So it's not supported by evidence. Reject as unsupported? Or duplicate? It's not a duplicate but the evidence is wrong. Could be unsupported. I'll reject as unsupported, because the evidence doesn't support the summary. Possibly the text was extracted incorrectly. So is_valid: false, reason: "Evidence quote does not match summary", category_if_not: "unsupported".

cand_a3178345033d932a: title "Dual-Duct VAV Zone Control Logic with Single Discharge Airflow Sensor", section 5.11.5.2. Summary describes similar logic. Evidence: "5.1.15.2.  Two runtime points shall be defined for each equipment:" Again, evidence doesn't match. Source pages [50]. Similar issue. Reject as unsupported.

cand_748935beef486c96: title "Cold-Deck Supply Air Temperature Override for Cooling Mode", section 5.11.5.1. Summary: If cold-deck SAT > room temp, active cold duct airflow setpoint shall be no higher than minimum endpoint. Evidence: "5.1.15.1.  Parallel equipment  shall be lead/lag or lead/standby rotated to maintain even wear." Not matching. Unsupported. Reject.

cand_97891d9ef5283ac2: title "Hot-Deck Supply Air Temperature Override for Heating Mode", section 5.11.5.1. Summary: If hot-deck SAT < room temp, active hot duct airflow setpoint shall be no higher than minimum endpoint. Evidence: "1. If hot-deck supply air temperature from the air handler is less than room temperature, the active hot duct airflow setpoint shall be no higher than the minimum endpoint." Evidence clearly matches summary. Source pages: [98]. This seems like a valid operational sequence item. Accept. is_valid: true.

cand_df84e5d8e54964d4: title "Overriding Logic to Avoid Backflow", section 5.11.5.3. Summary: If heating AH not proven ON, heating damper closed. If cooling AH not proven ON, cooling damper closed. Evidence: "a. If heating air handler is not proven ON, the heating damper shall be closed. b. If cooling air handler is not proven ON, the cooling damper shall be closed." Source pages [98, 102, 106, 109]. This matches. Accept.

cand_c0755564ea66c9fa: title "Low Airflow Alarm", section 5.11.6.1. Summary: thresholds and alarms. Evidence: "a. If the measured airflow is less than 70% of setpoint for 10 minutes while setpoint is greater than zero, generate a Level 4 alarm. b. If the measured airflow is less than 50% of setpoint for 10 minutes while setpoint is greater than zero, generate a Level 3 alarm. c. If a zone has an Importance-Multiplier of 0 (see Section 5.1.14.2.a.1) for its static pressure reset T&R control loop, low airflow alarms shall be suppressed for that zone." Evidence matches summary exactly. Source pages: [81, 86, 89, 98]. Valid fault diagnostic rule. Accept.

cand_0a616f4395ffacb4: title "Airflow Sensor Calibration Alarm", section 5.11.6.2. Summary: If fan serving zone off and airflow > max(10% max setpoint, 50 cfm) for 30 min, generate Level 3 alarm. Evidence: exact quote. Source pages [99, 102, 106, 109]. Accept.

cand_a7ceea338cfd2b5c: title "Leaking Damper Alarm", section 5.11.6.3. Summary: If damper position 0% and airflow > max(10% cooling max, 50 cfm) for 10 min while fan ON, generate Level 4. Evidence matches. Accept.

cand_9f0bfb5a9e39a3d3: title "Cooling SAT Reset Requests from Dual-Duct VAV Zone", section 5.11.8.1. Summary describes request sending logic based on temperature exceedance and loop. Evidence: "a. If the zone temperature exceeds the zone’s cooling setpoint by 3°C (5°F) for 2 minutes and after suppression period due to setpoint change per Section 5.1.20, send 3 requests. b. Else if the zone temperature exceeds the zone’s cooling setpoint by 2°C (3°F) for 2 minutes and after suppression period due to setpoint change per Section 5.1.20, send 2 requests. c. Else if the Cooling Loop is greater than 95%, send 1 request until the Cooling Loop is less than 85%. d. Else if the Cooling Loop is less than 95%, send 0 requests." Exact match. Source pages: [75, 83, 103, 107]. Accept.

cand_114c75638e9e8f24: title "Cold-Duct Static Pressure Reset Requests from Dual-Duct VAV Zone", section 5.11.8.2. Summary similar logic for static pressure reset. Evidence: exact quote matching summary. Accept.

cand_b9ab4c6b69f0087e: title "Endpoints as a Function of Zone Group Mode", section 5.11.4. Summary: Active endpoints vary by Zone Group mode. Evidence: "5.1.14.  Trim & Respond Set -Point Reset Logic" This is again a mismatched evidence. The summary talks about endpoints, but evidence is about Trim & Respond. Source pages: [46]. Possibly wrong evidence. The summary seems plausible but evidence doesn't support. Reject as unsupported or maybe it's an incomplete extraction? Since evidence is not matching, we must reject. Unsupported.

So for cand_520edf20988f53b5, cand_a3178345033d932a, cand_748935beef486c96, cand_b9ab4c6b69f0087e: reject as unsupported due to evidence mismatch. Others accept.

Let's output JSON with verdicts. For rejected ones: category_if_not "unsupported". For accepted ones, category_if_not null (or omitted). In output, we include "reason" and "category_if_not" if not null.

We must ensure the reply is only the JSON object. So construct accordingly.
